title: Strict TSConfig, TypeScript 7 Defaults, and Project Graphs · Contract Lens: Ts7 Default Changes
summary: Create a clean multi-package configuration that fails on unsafe boundaries, unused work, and incompatible module assumptions. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ts7 default changes increment in a strict TypeScript 7 project configuration. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Account for TypeScript 7 strict, target, module, rootDir, types, and side-effect import defaults without legacy no-op flags.
Use strict, noUncheckedIndexedAccess, exactOptionalPropertyTypes, useUnknownInCatchVariables, and override checks from actual risk.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
